channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Odisha Government Launches Crime Branch Inquiry Into Textbook Errors and FIR Filing
item.description: The Odisha government has directed the Crime Branch to investigate alleged factual inaccuracies, conceptual mistakes, and grammatical errors in state school textbooks. Following a high-level committee...

item.title: Nigerian Govt. Rescues Abducted Teachers and Students in Oyo State
item.description: The Nigerian government has successfully rescued over 50 teachers and students who were kidnapped by armed militants in Oyo State. The incident, which occurred on May 15, 2026, saw attackers on motorb...

item.title: Defense Secretary Teodoro Advocates for Enhanced Military Spending and Modernization
item.description: Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. emphasized the need to strengthen the Philippines' defense capabilities to protect its maritime rights in the South China Sea, particularly following the 2016 ar...

item.title: Shooting at Toronto’s Salsa Festival Leaves Two Dead, Several Injured
item.description: Toronto police confirmed two fatalities and multiple injuries following a shooting incident at the annual Salsa on St. Clair festival. The attack occurred shortly after 8 p.m. near the intersection of...

item.title: Philippines and Allies Reaffirm Arbitral Ruling Against China's South China Sea Claims
item.description: On the 10th anniversary of the 2016 Permanent Court of Arbitration ruling, the Philippines and 14 other nations reiterated their stance against China's maritime claims in the South China Sea. The join...

item.title: Geo News Resumes Transmission After 15-Day Suspension
item.description: Geo News, a Pakistani news channel, has resumed its transmission after a 15-day suspension imposed by the Pakistan Electronic Media Regulatory Authority (Pemra). The suspension was ordered due to the ...

item.title: Rachel Reeves Advises Andy Burnham on Governance and Economic Challenges
item.description: Rachel Reeves, the outgoing UK chancellor, has warned incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham that he must have a detailed plan for governing upon taking office. In an exclusive interview with the BBC, R...

item.title: Amber Alert Cancelled for Missing Alberta Boy, Investigation Continues
item.description: An Amber Alert for a missing six-year-old boy in Alberta has been cancelled, but the RCMP continues to investigate the abduction. Lanakai Morrison, the child in question, remains missing, and authorit...

item.title: South African footballer Jayden Adams, 25, dies weeks after playing in FIFA World Cup
item.description: Jayden Adams, a 25-year-old South African footballer who played in three matches at the FIFA World Cup, has died. His death was confirmed by Gayton McKenzie, South Africa's minister of sport, arts and...

item.title: Asking Friends Tough Questions Helps Build Deeper Connections
item.description: The article explores how asking friends uncomfortable or strange questions can help build stronger relationships and uncover hidden truths about them. It references Jungian relationship coach Dolly Mi...

item.title: BJP Nomination Dispute Sparks Clash Between Supporters and Police in Datia
item.description: Supporters of former Madhya Pradesh minister Narottam Mishra clashed with police in Datia district after the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) denied him a ticket for an upcoming assembly bypoll. Over 3,00...

item.title: Alvarez at Villaflor advance to PBO finals after strong performance
item.description: Jochelle Alvarez and Clarence Villaflor have made history by advancing to the finals of the Philippine National Badminton Open (PBO) 2026, marking their maiden appearance in the tournament's champions...

item.title: Daily Word Highlight: tiim-bagang for July 12, 2026
item.description: The article announces the Word of the Day for July 12, 2026, which is 'tiim-bagang'. This term, part of ABS-CBN's daily vocabulary series, aims to enrich language skills by introducing lesser-known wo...

item.title: Argentina's World Cup Prospects Amid Political Uncertainty
item.description: The article discusses Argentina's potential participation in the 2026 FIFA World Cup amid political turmoil following President Javier Milei's controversial leadership. While the national team remains...

item.title: Highway 1 Reopens in Fraser Canyon Amid Ongoing Wildfires
item.description: British Columbia Highway 1 through the Fraser Canyon has been reopened, but active wildfire operations continue in the area. The route, which had been closed since July 7 due to the rapid expansion of...

item.title: England Advances to World Cup Semifinals After Defeating Norway
item.description: England booked their place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-finals after a hard-fought 2-1 victory over Norway in extra time. Jude Bellingham starred with a brace, scoring both goals for the Three Lion...

item.title: Jack White offers Twin Temple opening slot after controversy over Satanic imagery
item.description: Twin Temple, an American band known for their satanic-themed music and retro doo-wop style, have been offered a support slot by Jack White following their removal from Charley Crockett's tour. The duo...

item.title: Experimental drug shows promise in reversing fatty liver disease through gut repair
item.description: An experimental drug called DT-109 has demonstrated the ability to reverse severe fatty liver disease (MASH) in animal studies by repairing the gut and preventing harmful toxins from damaging the live...

item.title: U.S. Launches Third Round of Strikes Against Iran Amid Escalating Tensions
item.description: The United States has launched a third round of strikes against Iran, marking the latest escalation in regional tensions. U.S. Central Command confirmed the attacks began around 7:30 ET on Saturday, r...

item.title: Two Arrested for Arson in Manipur Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Two individuals were arrested for allegedly setting fire to six abandoned houses in Manipur's Kanto Sabal village, sparking tensions between locals and security forces. The incident occurred on July 1...
